trivia_bullets.text: Lys Alana is the capital settlement of the Gilt Leaf tribe of elves on the plane of Lorwyn — the palace called Dawn's Light sits at its center, inhabited by Perfect and Exquisite class elves who treat beauty as a social hierarchy. Bowmasters defending this seat would represent the outer guard of that aristocratic order. [MTG Wiki: Lys Alana, via WebSearch snippet]

Lys Alana Bowmaster originated in Morningtide (February 2008), the follow-up small set to Lorwyn. The 'whenever you cast an Elf spell' trigger was a signature Morningtide design pattern: the set shifted Lorwyn's tribal theme from 'creatures in play' to 'spells cast,' rewarding players for playing the tribal game more actively. Commander Legends (November 2020) is its only reprint. [Scryfall prints search]

Reach plus a flying-damage trigger on every Elf cast gives the card two distinct anti-air angles in Elf tribal Commander builds. EDHREC rank 16400 confirms it is a fringe inclusion — the 2-damage trigger is conditional on the flying creature being a legal target, and most Commander elf builds prioritize creature density over disruption. Legal in Modern, Legacy, Pauper, and Commander formats. [Scryfall legalities; Scryfall edhrec_rank field]

Multiple named cards reference Lys Alana as a location: Lys Alana Huntmaster (Lorwyn), Lys Alana Scarblade (Morningtide), and Lys Alana Dignitary (Lorwyn Eclipsed). The Bowmaster is the Morningtide entry in that named-place cycle, grounding a mechanical action (shooting flyers) in a specific seat of Lorwyn elf power. [Scryfall card search]
